element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Tutorial phase; satellites orbit automatically or can be fixed in position via A button toggle
Replaces default gun; obtained from destroyed containers
Replaces current weapon; area-coverage offensive option
Replaces current weapon; high-damage offensive option
Enhances player movement velocity; obtained from containers

element_introduction_order.element: Life recovery item (R item - US/EU versions only)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: 1-up; restores one hit point; absent in Japanese version
Stage-specific patterns; culmination of stage difficulty
Shifts from horizontal auto-scroll to vertical auto-scroll; requires directional weapon adjustment

typical_tension_triggers: weapon loss on hit (JP version only)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Continuous horizontal/vertical auto-scroll creates steady forward pressure; intensity builds through enemy density and formation complexity; boss encounters provide climactic tension peaks

spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Hidden level select accessible via Controller 2 button codes at title screen (varies by version)

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introductory stage with basic enemy patterns; player learns satellite positioning and weapon switching mechanics; Japanese version starts with only 3 hit-points vs 6 in US/EU versions
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Stages 2-4 introduce mixed enemy formations, stage transitions (horizontal to vertical scrolling), and mid-tier weapon power-ups; difficulty increases through enemy aggression variation based on stage number
difficulty_curve.late_game: Stage 5 represents final challenge with increased enemy density; boss difficulty escalates; satellite and weapon mastery required for survival
notable_spikes: Stage 3/4 transition (Astrotube stage; US/EU ordering differs from JP version)
Battleship stage has notably more aggressive minor enemy patterns in US/EU versions
Astrotube stage less aggressive in US/EU versions than JP version
Boss encounters at each stage end

notable_level_patterns.description: Shifts to vertical scrolling (upward), requiring player to adapt aiming from horizontal-only weapon fire constraints; represents major spatial grammar shift midway through game
Industrial/mechanical aesthetic; high enemy aggression in US/EU versions; tests sustained weapon management and satellite positioning under pressure
Early stages featuring metropolitan environments with standard enemy waves; gradient difficulty introduction
